rulings.Case Title: SECURITY NATIONAL VS STATE OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff/Petitioner Security National Guaranty, Inc.'S Motion For Entry Of A Judgment And Writ
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, MAY 27, 2008 LINE 6. PLAINTIFF SECURITY NATIONAL GUARANTY, INC.'S MOTION FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT AND A WRIT: HEARING REQUIRED. IT APPEARS THAT THE PARTIES INTEND TO REACH AGREEMENT ON COSTS. ON THE ISSUE OF ATTORNEYS' FEES, IT WOULD APPEAR SUFFICIENT SIMPLY TO STATE THAT PETITIONER MAY SEEK TO RECOVER REASONABLE ATTORNEYS' FEES AGAINST THE COMMISSION. AS TO THE WRIT, IT APPEARS THAT PETITIONERS' SUBPARTS (1) TO (3) OF THE THIRD PARAGRAPH ARE CONSISTENT WITH THE DECISION OF THE COURT OF APPEALS AND THE PARTIES UNDERSTANDING THAT A MODIFIED PROPOSAL WILL BE SUBMITTED. ON THE ISSUE OF THE HEARING, THIS MAY BE EFFECTIVELY ADDRESSED BY SIMPLY KEEPING THE JANUARY 31, 2009 RETURN DATE ON THE WRIT. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
